subject: Mathematics
title: Triangle Inequality
description: Learn Triangle Inequality in one minute.

hook.text: Can you build a triangle from any three sticks? Not always.

buildup.text: The triangle inequality says each side must be shorter than the sum of the other two.

discovery.text: Sticks of length 1, 2, and 10 can't form a triangle. The short sides can't reach each other.

twist.text: This rule extends beyond geometry. It governs distances in GPS, networks, and machine learning.

climax.text: Any system that measures distance must obey the triangle inequality or produce nonsense.

punchline.text: Not every trio of lengths can close into a shape.

quiz.question: Can sides of length 2, 3, and 8 form a triangle?
options: Only if the angle is right
